speaker: other
text: Congratulations!!! After careful consideration you have been confirmed qualified for the position of a Remote Product Design Manager due to your level of experience and working skills. Therefore the Company has decided to hire you as one of our New working staff. Welcome to Splunk Technology!On August 5, I got an email invitation to interview for a Remote Product Design Manager position at Splunk from info@splunkcareers.us to my university email. It states that I was fast-tracked to an interview because my AngelList profile shows my skills and experience would be a great fit. I scheduled the interview with an alleged Splunk HR member (Matt Olsen). I even looked up his profile to make sure that I was talking to a real person from the company. After the Skype chat interview, I was told to be online on Monday at 10 am for updates. On Monday, I received the offer, and a request to fill and sign the employment contract, background check authorization, direct deposit form to fill and sign, and a copy of my driver's license.Later that week, I had a chat with the alleged CIO of Splunk (Alexander Fridman) who briefed me on equipment that will be sent to set up my home office. I was told I would be given company funds to purchase an iPhone 13 Pro, an Apple Watch Series 7, and Microsoft Business Standard. The finance department will send me the company account information to purchase the items. I was instructed to link the company account to my card to pay off the current balance before I make any purchases. At this point, I received a temporary work email from IT department which I had to log in via privateemail(dot)com where I will receive updates.On Monday, August 15, I received all the information regarding the bank account and successfully linked it to my card. I use the account to pay off the outstanding balance as told and went to the Apple Store to purchase the items. I was instructed to send the items I purchased to the vendor's address so that proper company branding and the most up-to-date software can be installed for security purposes. I went to the UPS store on the same day and sent off the package via Next Day shipping.While driving home, I felt uneasy so I called my friends and family to consult my suspicion. One friend suggested that I reach out to HR employees at Splunk on LinkedIn to be sure. An HR employee confirmed my suspicion. I immediately called UPS to cancel my shipment, froze my credit card, and reported the identity theft incident to the FTC. (Thank you! UPS)During this interaction with the people who were pretending to be employees at Splunk, I felt like I was being welcomed into the team. These scammers used phrases like 'You're welcome, Splunker!' 'Have a great day, Splunker' to make me feel like these people were real employees.The most horrifying thing is how I receive this through my school email. They were targeting students like me. A part of me felt like I should have known better. The other part of me knows this is not one of those silly scams. This is an elaborate, calculated, and targeted crime.

speaker: other
text: Congratulations!!! After careful consideration, you have been confirmed qualified for the Remote Product Design Manager position based on your level of experience and professional skills. This is why the company has decided to hire you among our new employees. Welcome to Splunk Technology! On August 5, I received an email invitation to interview for a remote product design manager position at Splunk, from info@splunkcareers.us at my university email address. It says I was fast-tracked to an interview because my AngelList profile shows my skills and experience would be a good fit. I scheduled the interview with a presumed member of Splunk HR (Matt Olsen). I even looked at his profile to make sure I was talking to a real person at the company. After the Skype chat interview, I was told to be online at 10 a.m. Monday for updates. On Monday, I received the offer along with a request to fill out and sign the employment contract, a background check authorization, a direct deposit form to fill out and sign, and a copy of my driver's license. Later in the week I had a conversation with the alleged CIO of Splunk (Alexander Fridman) who informed me of the equipment that will be sent to set up my home office. I was told I would receive funds from the company to purchase an iPhone 13 Pro, Apple Watch Series 7, and Microsoft Business Standard. The finance department will send me the company account information to purchase the items. I was asked to link the company account to my card to pay the current balance before making any purchases. At this point I received a temporary work email from IT which I had to log in to via privateemail(dot)com where I will receive updates. On Monday, August 15, I received all the bank account information and successfully linked it to my card. I used the account to pay the outstanding balance as instructed and went to the Apple Store to purchase the items. I was asked to send the items I purchased to the seller's address so that proper company branding and the latest software could be installed for security purposes. I went to the UPS store the same day and sent the package via next day shipping. When I got home, I felt uneasy, so I called my friends and family to consult my suspicions. A friend suggested I contact Splunk HR people on LinkedIn to find out. A human resources employee confirmed my suspicions. I immediately called UPS to cancel my shipment, froze my credit card, and reported the identity theft incident to the FTC. (Thanks! UPS) During this interaction with the people posing as Splunk employees, I felt like I was welcomed into the team. These scammers used phrases like “You’re welcome, Splunker! » “Have a nice day, Splunker” to make me feel like these people were real employees. The most horrible thing is how I receive this via email at school. They targeted students like me. Part of me felt like I should have known better. The other part of me knows this isn't one of those stupid scams. This is an elaborate, calculated and targeted crime.
